explanations.1: This checks if Apache has the mod_headers module installed; everything inside only runs if it does.
explanations.4: This sets a custom header called 'Protected-by' — the comment suggests you can either put a value here or delete this line entirely.
explanations.7: This line sets permissions for what browser features (like camera or microphone) are allowed; replace XXXXXXXXXXXXXX with your actual domain name.
explanations.21: The condition at the end (after the quotes) means this header only gets sent when the connection is using HTTPS — it won't apply on regular http.
explanations.40: This entire line is commented out as a safety measure because Content-Security-Policy can easily break a site if configured wrong; uncomment and test thoroughly before using it.
